commit.message:
fix(ci): move Register Full Flow test to end of suite to prevent cascade failures (#2693)

Registration creates an encrypted SQLCipher database with CPU-intensive
key derivation, 58 tables, and 500+ settings, which blocks the
single-threaded CI server for 2+ minutes. When this test ran mid-suite,
the 3 tests immediately after it (Research Submit, Export Functionality,
Concurrent Limit) couldn't even login because the server was
unresponsive, causing cascade failures.

Moving it to the very end ensures no other tests are impacted.

commit.message:
fix: skip sentence-splitter tests when HuggingFace Hub is unreachable (#2689)

The two sentence-splitter tests download a model from huggingface.co at
runtime.  In the Docker CI environment the Hub returns 403, causing
hard failures.  Add a lightweight reachability check and skip the tests
when the Hub is not available.

commit.message:
fix: resolve Safari CI test failures from duplicate scripts and mobile overflow (#2682)

Remove duplicate script loading (formatting.js, api.js, socket.js, urls.js)
from page templates that base.html already loads globally. The second load
caused SyntaxError on const API_BASE_URL redeclaration in Safari/WebKit.

Add redeclaration guard to api.js (var + typeof check) as defense-in-depth.

Add mobile CSS overrides for settings page elements that exceed 390px viewport
(label max-width, grid minmax values, section title nowrap). Also add min-width
override for research page ldr-half form groups.

fix: align CSRF token pattern in research API test with codebase conventions (#2671)

- Switch from external page.$eval + parameter passing (Pattern A) to
  inline document.querySelector inside page.evaluate (Pattern B),
  matching the 9+ other CSRF-protected POST calls in the test suite
- Remove incorrect "CSRF-exempt" comment in flask_api.py — the
  /research/api blueprint is NOT exempt (only api_v1 is)

commit.message:
fix: remove duplicate api.js script tags and correct library test selectors (#2680)

* fix: remove duplicate api.js script tags and correct library test selectors

- Remove redundant api.js <script> from progress, results, history, and
  benchmark pages (already loaded in base.html), fixing
  'API_BASE_URL has already been declared' console errors
- Update library header stats test to use #total-pdfs and #total-size
  ID selectors matching the actual HTML structure

* fix: remove duplicate script tags and fix UI test bugs

App fixes:
- Remove duplicate formatting.js from progress, results, history, benchmark
  (already loaded in base.html)
- Remove duplicate socket.js from progress, benchmark
  (already loaded in base.html)

Test fixes:
- Fix modelProviderSetting crash: check element is <select> before
  accessing .options (prevents TypeError on non-select matches)
- Fix library documents & news feed 300s timeouts: use domcontentloaded
  instead of networkidle2 (websockets/auto-refresh prevent idle)
- Fix register username test: expect 1-char username to fail minlength=3
  check, not pass it
- Fix register password strength test: use "abcdefg" (score=1, weak)
  instead of "weakpass" (score=2, medium)

* fix: correct selectors and timing in UI tests to reduce skipped tests

History page:
- Wait for JS-rendered items before checking DOM (items load via API)
- Add .ldr-history-item selector matching actual DOM structure

Settings interactions:
- Add .ldr-settings-item selector for number inputs, dropdowns, toggles
  (tests used .settings/.setting but actual class is .ldr-settings-item)
- Add fallback selectors: input[type="number"][name], select[name],
  input[type="checkbox"][name]
- Add delay for settings search to wait for dynamic content load

Library documents:
- Add #search-documents to search filter selector

Research form:
- Support custom dropdown component for search engine selector
  (app uses .ldr-custom-dropdown, not <select>)

* ci: remove UI test retry-on-failure logic

The retry doubled CI time (~20+ min wasted on 300s timeouts and full
re-run). With the test fixes in this branch the root causes are
addressed directly, making the retry unnecessary.

* ci: clean up run_tests wrapper after retry removal

* fix: remove unreachable dead code in history table test

commit.message:
fix: E2E password validation alignment & missing CSRF token (#2674)

* fix: align E2E test passwords and client-side validation with PasswordValidator requirements

The PasswordValidator added in #2594 requires uppercase, lowercase, and
digit characters but E2E test passwords lacked uppercase letters, causing
registration to fail server-side and cascading auth failures across all
SSRF protection tests. Also aligns the client-side registration form
validation with the server-side rules so users get immediate feedback,
and fixes a stale-variable bug in ensureLoggedIn after post-registration
login.

* fix: align change-password page requirements and validation with PasswordValidator

The change-password page only listed "8 characters" but the server-side
PasswordValidator also requires uppercase, lowercase, and digit. Users
would submit a password meeting the displayed rules only to get a
confusing server rejection. Updated requirements text and added
client-side validation to match.

* fix: make PasswordValidator single source of truth for password rules

Remove duplicated password validation logic from JavaScript. Client-side
forms now call POST /auth/validate-password API endpoint which runs
PasswordValidator.validate_strength() server-side and returns errors as
JSON. Template requirement lists are rendered via Jinja from
PasswordValidator.get_requirements(). This eliminates the JS/Python
desync that caused E2E test failures.

* fix: handle non-OK HTTP responses in validatePasswordViaAPI

Check resp.ok before parsing JSON to gracefully handle CSRF token
expiry (400) and server errors (500). Falls back to letting
server-side validation catch issues on form submit.

* refactor: extract validatePasswordViaAPI to shared JS file and unify strength bar

- Create auth-validation.js with IIFE pattern (matches safe-logger.js, safe-fetch.js)
- Remove duplicate inline validatePasswordViaAPI from register.html and change_password.html
- Add csrfToken parameter since different pages have different token values
- Remove legacy onkeyup="checkPasswordStrength()" from change_password.html input
- Merge strength bar logic into addEventListener('input') handler (consistent with register.html)

* fix: resolve 3 UI bugs found in security review

- change_password.html: clear is-invalid class and error text when
  password field is emptied (error state was persisting)
- register.html: add setFieldValid() call in blur handler so valid
  passwords get green border feedback
- auth-validation.js: use const instead of var for consistency with
  other security JS files

* fix: align username client-side validation with server-side rules

- Set minlength="3" (was "1") to match server's 3-char minimum
- Add length check in input handler so users see error while typing
- Add full validation in blur handler (length + pattern + valid state)
- Update hint text to mention the 3-character minimum

* fix: align strength bar with PasswordValidator's 4 requirements

The bar now checks the same 4 rules as PasswordValidator (length >= 8,
uppercase, lowercase, digit) instead of 6 heuristic factors. This
prevents the bar from showing green when validation would actually fail,
or staying yellow when all requirements are met.

* fix: add missing CSRF token to E2E research start test

The test for /research/api/start was failing with 400 "CSRF token is
missing" because it incorrectly assumed the endpoint was CSRF-exempt.
Add getCSRFToken import and pass X-CSRFToken header, matching the
pattern already used for the terminate test.

missing" because it incorrectly assumed the endpoint was CSRF-exempt.

pattern already used for the terminate test.

commit.message:
fix: follow-up fixes from PR #2654 body-guard review (#2662)

* fix: remove vacuous is_intermediate_redirect guard in SafeSession.send()

The `kwargs.get("allow_redirects", True)` clause always evaluated to True
because requests.Session.send() pops the key before our code runs. Remove
the entire is_intermediate_redirect guard and call _check_response_size()
unconditionally — it only inspects Content-Length, which is harmless on
redirect responses (they rarely have one, and checking is a cheap header read).

Adds 6 SafeSession-specific tests covering size checks on final responses,
redirect chains, missing headers, and multi-value Content-Length.

* security: enforce body-size limit for chunked/missing Content-Length responses

_check_response_size() previously only inspected the Content-Length header.
Responses using chunked transfer encoding or missing Content-Length entirely
had no size enforcement, allowing unbounded memory consumption.

Adds _install_body_guard() which wraps response.raw.read() with a bounded
reader that tracks cumulative bytes and raises ValueError when
MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE is exceeded. Automatically installed by
_check_response_size() when Content-Length is absent or unparseable.

Covers all access patterns: .content, .text, .json(), and
stream=True with .iter_content().

Adds 6 tests for body guard: within-limit, exceeds-limit (chunked and
non-chunked), CL-present no-op, and streamed cumulative enforcement.

* fix: remove early-return guard in _install_body_guard for invalid Content-Length

_install_body_guard had a defensive `if content_length: return` that
silently skipped wrapping when Content-Length was present but invalid
(e.g. "abc", "-1", ","). Since _check_response_size calls the guard
precisely for those invalid cases, the early-return made it a no-op,
leaving those responses unprotected.

- Remove the early-return guard so the function always installs
- Update docstring to clarify callers decide when to call
- Add tests for invalid and negative Content-Length getting the guard
- Add resp.close assertion to test_missing_cl_exceeds_limit

* fix: follow-up fixes from PR #2654 body-guard review

- Route test_invalid_cl and test_negative_cl through _check_response_size
  instead of calling _install_body_guard directly (tests were tautological)
- Fix error message: "no Content-Length header" → "Content-Length absent or invalid"
- Update _check_response_size docstring to cover all guard-install triggers
- Clarify _install_body_guard docstring re: caller responsibility

commit.message:
fix: increase UI test timeout, fix Playwright password, add registration debugging (#2676)

* fix: increase UI test timeout to 120min, fix Playwright password, add registration debugging

- Increase timeout-minutes from 45 to 120 in puppeteer-e2e-tests.yml and docker-tests.yml
- Fix Playwright test password from testpass123 to Testpass123 (meets PasswordValidator uppercase requirement)
- Add init_test_database.py step to playwright-webkit-tests.yml for both desktop and mobile Safari jobs
- Add server-side registration error logging in auth routes.py
- Add client-side error message capture in Puppeteer registerUser helper

* fix: only log registration validation errors in CI environment

* fix: update all remaining test passwords to meet PasswordValidator requirements

The new PasswordValidator (from #2594) requires at least one uppercase
letter. Update testpass123 → Testpass123 in all 12 remaining test files
that were missed in the initial commit.

Also fix review findings:
- Use sanitize_for_log(username) in registration warning (matches login pattern)
- Fix Puppeteer error capture CSS selectors (.alert-danger → .alert)

* fix: remove uppercase password requirement per NIST SP 800-63B guidelines

The uppercase letter requirement in PasswordValidator adds user friction
without meaningful security gain. NIST guidelines explicitly recommend
against composition rules. This removes the [A-Z] check from the backend
validator, frontend strength bars, and all tests, while reverting test
passwords from Testpass123 back to testpass123.

* fix: regenerate golden master settings after Exa search engine merge

The Exa search engine PR (#2660) added new search.engine.web.exa.*
keys that the golden master JSON didn't include yet.

* fix: remove CI-only registration debug logging

The debug logging was added to diagnose the uppercase password issue,
which is now resolved. Remove it to avoid logging usernames on
registration failure.

commit.message:
fix: complete path-hardening follow-ups from PRs #2646, #2648 (#2672)

* fix: complete path-hardening follow-ups from review of PRs #2646, #2648

- download_service.py: .exists() → .is_file() in is_already_downloaded()
- test_download_service.py: update mock from .exists to .is_file
- test_library_service_crud_deep.py: hardcoded sentinels → constants
- env_settings.py: add .resolve() to bootstrap path expansion

* fix: move .resolve() after expandvars to prevent mangled env-var paths

When path_str contains $HOME (not ~), .resolve() was called before
expandvars, treating $HOME as a literal directory name and prepending
cwd — producing wrong paths like /cwd/$HOME/dir. Move .resolve() to
after expandvars so env vars are expanded first. Also strengthen the
test assertion to check the exact expected path.

commit.message:
fix: address review issues in Exa search engine (#2075) (#2660)

* Add Exa.ai search engine implementation

Implement ExaSearchEngine class with neural search capabilities

Support for auto, neural, keyword, fast, and deep search types

Include domain filtering, date filtering, and category options

Add full content retrieval with text, highlights, and summaries

Register Exa in default_settings.json with complete configuration

Add comprehensive test suite for Exa search engine

Follow BaseSearchEngine pattern similar to Tavily implementation

Co-Authored-By: Warp <agent@warp.dev>

* fix: address review issues in Exa search engine implementation

- Restore bearer:disable SAST comment removed from module_whitelist.py
- Remove invalid "keyword" search type (not supported by Exa API)
- Fix misleading error message referencing non-existent EXA_API_KEY env var
- Parse displayed_link to domain only (consistent with Serper engine)
- Remove dead if/pass code block in _get_full_content

* test: add tests for displayed_link parsing, optional fields, and rate limit detection

* fix: remove dead code and fix Exa search engine content retrieval

- Remove dead ExaWrapper class and FullSearchResults integration that
  always raised AttributeError (FullSearchResults has no _get_full_content)
- Request highlights and summary from Exa API so parsing code works
- Add type safety check on highlights field (must be list)
- Simplify _get_full_content with _extract_result helper (fixes bug in
  snippet-only path where _full_result wasn't cleaned from copies)
- Remove redundant SEARCH_SNIPPETS_ONLY check (base class already gates)
- Remove dead self._search_results storage and cleanup
- Add tests for contents payload, _extract_result, and highlights type safety

* fix: address 6 review issues in Exa search engine integration

- Forward settings_snapshot to super().__init__() for thread-isolated config
- Use _resolve_api_key() instead of inline key resolution (validates placeholders)
- Replace deprecated Exa highlights fields (highlightsPerUrl, numSentences) with maxCharacters
- Use base class _raise_if_rate_limit() for 429 status and exception handling
- Delete duplicate _extract_result/_get_full_content (base class provides identical behavior)
- Update tests: 3 deleted (tested removed methods), 3 updated for new patterns

---------

Co-authored-by: NimbleAINinja <graham.vosloo@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Warp <agent@warp.dev>

and data.phase) which should be escaped as a defense-in-depth measure.

commit.message:
fix: make PasswordValidator single source of truth for password rules (#2664)

* fix: align E2E test passwords and client-side validation with PasswordValidator requirements

The PasswordValidator added in #2594 requires uppercase, lowercase, and
digit characters but E2E test passwords lacked uppercase letters, causing
registration to fail server-side and cascading auth failures across all
SSRF protection tests. Also aligns the client-side registration form
validation with the server-side rules so users get immediate feedback,
and fixes a stale-variable bug in ensureLoggedIn after post-registration
login.

* fix: align change-password page requirements and validation with PasswordValidator

The change-password page only listed "8 characters" but the server-side
PasswordValidator also requires uppercase, lowercase, and digit. Users
would submit a password meeting the displayed rules only to get a
confusing server rejection. Updated requirements text and added
client-side validation to match.

* fix: make PasswordValidator single source of truth for password rules

Remove duplicated password validation logic from JavaScript. Client-side
forms now call POST /auth/validate-password API endpoint which runs
PasswordValidator.validate_strength() server-side and returns errors as
JSON. Template requirement lists are rendered via Jinja from
PasswordValidator.get_requirements(). This eliminates the JS/Python
desync that caused E2E test failures.

* fix: handle non-OK HTTP responses in validatePasswordViaAPI

Check resp.ok before parsing JSON to gracefully handle CSRF token
expiry (400) and server errors (500). Falls back to letting
server-side validation catch issues on form submit.

* refactor: extract validatePasswordViaAPI to shared JS file and unify strength bar

- Create auth-validation.js with IIFE pattern (matches safe-logger.js, safe-fetch.js)
- Remove duplicate inline validatePasswordViaAPI from register.html and change_password.html
- Add csrfToken parameter since different pages have different token values
- Remove legacy onkeyup="checkPasswordStrength()" from change_password.html input
- Merge strength bar logic into addEventListener('input') handler (consistent with register.html)

* fix: resolve 3 UI bugs found in security review

- change_password.html: clear is-invalid class and error text when
  password field is emptied (error state was persisting)
- register.html: add setFieldValid() call in blur handler so valid
  passwords get green border feedback
- auth-validation.js: use const instead of var for consistency with
  other security JS files

* fix: align username client-side validation with server-side rules

- Set minlength="3" (was "1") to match server's 3-char minimum
- Add length check in input handler so users see error while typing
- Add full validation in blur handler (length + pattern + valid state)
- Update hint text to mention the 3-character minimum

* fix: align strength bar with PasswordValidator's 4 requirements

The bar now checks the same 4 rules as PasswordValidator (length >= 8,
uppercase, lowercase, digit) instead of 6 heuristic factors. This
prevents the bar from showing green when validation would actually fail,
or staying yellow when all requirements are met.

commit.message:
fix: complete ldr- prefix migration for remaining selectors in settings.js

Fix missed querySelector/querySelectorAll calls that still used unprefixed
class names after the initial rename, which caused:
- Data loss: JSON property controls (e.g. model configs) silently saved as {}
- Broken reset: expanded JSON controls not visually reset
- Missing styles: empty-state divs unstyled

Changes:
- .json-property-control → .ldr-json-property-control (3 query selectors)
- .json-property-item → .ldr-json-property-item (1 closest() call)
- checkbox template class: json-property-control → ldr-json-property-control
- empty-state → ldr-empty-state (2 HTML templates)
- Revert accidental 1-space indentation drift in 3 locations

fix: refresh provider status after embedding provider or Ollama URL change (#2656)

* fix: refresh provider status after embedding provider or Ollama URL change

Auto-saving the provider dropdown or Ollama URL did not refresh the
provider status indicators, so "Not reachable" / "Ready" would stay
stale until the full form was submitted or page reloaded. Now calls
loadAvailableModels() after these auto-saves to update status immediately.

* fix: auto-save embedding model on provider change and clean reindex

1. Dispatch change event on model select after provider change so
   the new model is persisted to DB (guarded by autoSaveListenersAttached).

2. On force_reindex, wipe old chunks, FAISS files, and RAGIndex records
   upfront via CascadeHelper before re-indexing. This prevents mixed-model
   vectors if reindex is cancelled midway.

commit.message:
fix: remove early-return guard in _install_body_guard for invalid Content-Length (#2654)

* fix: remove vacuous is_intermediate_redirect guard in SafeSession.send()

The `kwargs.get("allow_redirects", True)` clause always evaluated to True
because requests.Session.send() pops the key before our code runs. Remove
the entire is_intermediate_redirect guard and call _check_response_size()
unconditionally — it only inspects Content-Length, which is harmless on
redirect responses (they rarely have one, and checking is a cheap header read).

Adds 6 SafeSession-specific tests covering size checks on final responses,
redirect chains, missing headers, and multi-value Content-Length.

* security: enforce body-size limit for chunked/missing Content-Length responses

_check_response_size() previously only inspected the Content-Length header.
Responses using chunked transfer encoding or missing Content-Length entirely
had no size enforcement, allowing unbounded memory consumption.

Adds _install_body_guard() which wraps response.raw.read() with a bounded
reader that tracks cumulative bytes and raises ValueError when
MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE is exceeded. Automatically installed by
_check_response_size() when Content-Length is absent or unparseable.

Covers all access patterns: .content, .text, .json(), and
stream=True with .iter_content().

Adds 6 tests for body guard: within-limit, exceeds-limit (chunked and
non-chunked), CL-present no-op, and streamed cumulative enforcement.

* fix: remove early-return guard in _install_body_guard for invalid Content-Length

_install_body_guard had a defensive `if content_length: return` that
silently skipped wrapping when Content-Length was present but invalid
(e.g. "abc", "-1", ","). Since _check_response_size calls the guard
precisely for those invalid cases, the early-return made it a no-op,
leaving those responses unprotected.

- Remove the early-return guard so the function always installs
- Update docstring to clarify callers decide when to call
- Add tests for invalid and negative Content-Length getting the guard
- Add resp.close assertion to test_missing_cl_exceeds_limit

without canonicalization. This prevents late path traversal via symlinks
or relative components like '../' in admin-configured storage paths.

Locations: library_service.py, library_routes.py, search_engine_library.py,
search_engine_collection.py.

commit.message:
security: enforce body-size limit for chunked/missing Content-Length (#2644)

* fix: remove vacuous is_intermediate_redirect guard in SafeSession.send()

The `kwargs.get("allow_redirects", True)` clause always evaluated to True
because requests.Session.send() pops the key before our code runs. Remove
the entire is_intermediate_redirect guard and call _check_response_size()
unconditionally — it only inspects Content-Length, which is harmless on
redirect responses (they rarely have one, and checking is a cheap header read).

Adds 6 SafeSession-specific tests covering size checks on final responses,
redirect chains, missing headers, and multi-value Content-Length.

* security: enforce body-size limit for chunked/missing Content-Length responses

_check_response_size() previously only inspected the Content-Length header.
Responses using chunked transfer encoding or missing Content-Length entirely
had no size enforcement, allowing unbounded memory consumption.

Adds _install_body_guard() which wraps response.raw.read() with a bounded
reader that tracks cumulative bytes and raises ValueError when
MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE is exceeded. Automatically installed by
_check_response_size() when Content-Length is absent or unparseable.

Covers all access patterns: .content, .text, .json(), and
stream=True with .iter_content().

Adds 6 tests for body guard: within-limit, exceeds-limit (chunked and
non-chunked), CL-present no-op, and streamed cumulative enforcement.

commit.message:
fix: handle SSE auth errors in library and download manager frontends (#2616)

* fix: handle SSE auth errors in library and download manager frontends

Three SSE completion handlers checked `data.complete` without first
checking `data.error`, causing auth errors to be silently swallowed.
Apply the same error-guard pattern already used by `downloadAllNew()`
and `downloadAllAsText()` in download_manager.html.

* refactor: extract shared handleSSECompletion utility from duplicated blocks

The identical SSE error/completion handling block was copy-pasted in 5
locations across download_manager.html and library.html. Extract it into
a shared utility function in sse-completion.js to eliminate duplication.

commit.message:
refactor: path traversal protection cleanup — sentinel constants, symlink checks, bug fixes (#2615)

* security: add path traversal protection for file reads

Add PathValidator.validate_safe_path() checks when resolving relative
paths to absolute paths for file reads. This prevents path traversal
attacks where malicious file_path values could read files outside the
library directory.

Fixed locations:
- PDFStorageManager: has_pdf, _load_from_filesystem, delete_pdf
- DownloadService: _try_extract_from_existing_pdf
- utils: get_absolute_library_path, get_absolute_path_from_settings

* fix: apply ruff formatting

* fix: standardize error handling for path traversal protection

Address AI code review feedback by making error handling consistent:

- Change utility functions to return Optional[Path] instead of raising
  ValueError, matching PDFStorageManager._get_safe_file_path() pattern
- Update all callers in library_service.py and document_deletion.py to
  handle None return values safely
- Add _get_safe_absolute_path helper to LibraryService for cleaner code
- Prevents crashes when path traversal is blocked, instead gracefully
  handling invalid paths

This makes the security protection non-breaking for existing code while
still logging and blocking path traversal attempts.

* test: add comprehensive tests for path traversal protection

Add 24 tests covering:
- get_absolute_library_path: valid paths, traversal attempts, edge cases
- get_absolute_path_from_settings: valid paths, traversal attempts
- LibraryService._get_safe_absolute_path: markers, edge cases, traversal
- PDFStorageManager._get_safe_file_path: all path scenarios
- Integration tests: has_pdf, _load_from_filesystem, delete_pdf

These tests verify that malicious file_path values in the database cannot
be used to read or delete files outside the library directory.

* fix: handle None in is_already_downloaded path check

Found by code review: is_already_downloaded() could crash with
AttributeError when absolute_path was None (path traversal blocked).

Changes:
- Add None check before calling .exists() on absolute_path
- Only mark tracker as not downloaded if path exists but file doesn't
- Add test case for malicious path in download tracker

* test: add comprehensive path traversal attack vector tests

Add 16 more tests covering:
- Various path traversal attack patterns (simple .., nested, absolute)
- Edge cases (whitespace, special chars, unicode, long paths)
- CascadeHelper path safety (special markers, None, empty, file ops)
- Verification that traversal staying within root is allowed

Total: 41 tests for path traversal protection

* test: add OWASP-recommended path traversal test vectors

Based on OWASP and PayloadsAllTheThings research, add tests for:
- Windows-style backslash traversal (platform-aware)
- Mixed slash patterns with real traversal
- Mangled patterns (..../) - verified they stay in library
- Null byte injection (%00)
- UNC path injection (Windows network shares)
- Dot segment variations (..;/ Tomcat bypass)

Total: 47 tests for path traversal protection

Sources:
- OWASP Path Traversal: https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Path_Traversal
- OWASP Testing Guide: https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide
- PayloadsAllTheThings: https://github.com/swisskyrepo/PayloadsAllTheThings

* test: add tests for PDF storage path traversal protection

Tests _validate_filesystem_path blocks traversal, symlinks, and sentinel
values. Verifies library_root is resolved and get_absolute_path_from_settings
rejects paths escaping library root.

* fix: resolve 5 test bugs in path traversal protection

- Rename _validate_filesystem_path → _get_safe_file_path (method name mismatch)
- Change test_traversal_raises_value_error to test_traversal_returns_none (function returns None, doesn't raise)
- Resolve library_root on init for symlink defense-in-depth
- Add symlink detection in _get_safe_file_path (werkzeug safe_join only checks string traversal)
- Fix PR reference in docstring (#1943 → #1890)

* fix: correct 2 test bugs found in post-fix review of path traversal protection

Bug 6: inspect.getsource target updated from has_pdf to _get_safe_file_path
since sentinel strings were moved during refactor.

Bug 7: Added file_type="pdf" and DocumentBlob mock to has_pdf path traversal
test so it exercises the actual path validation code instead of short-circuiting
at the file_type check.

* fix: resolve 3 low-severity bugs found in review round 2

Bug 8: _infer_storage_mode now excludes 'text_only_not_stored' sentinel
Bug 9: test_null_byte_injection asserts None instead of permissive check
Bug 10: Remove dead code after validate_safe_path (always raises on failure)

* refactor: define sentinel constants and harden path validation

- Define FILE_PATH_SENTINELS constants in constants.py, replacing
  hardcoded "metadata_only", "text_only_not_stored", "blob_deleted"
  strings across 8 production files and 7 test files
- Add "blob_deleted" to sentinel filters in _get_safe_file_path and
  _get_safe_absolute_path (prevents constructing invalid paths)
- Add "blob_deleted" to _infer_storage_mode sentinel check
- Fix line 342 bug: use FILE_PATH_SENTINELS (was only checking
  metadata_only) and fall back to doc.filename instead of hardcoded
  string
- Add symlink checks in get_absolute_library_path and
  get_absolute_path_from_settings (not in cascade deletion)
- Fix .exists() → .is_file() in 3 pdf_storage_manager locations
- Add .resolve() after .expanduser() in 4 library_root locations
- Remove hardcoded /tmp in test_pdf_path_traversal.py (use tmp_path)
- Add 4 new tests: blob_deleted sentinel, infer_storage_mode, and
  2 symlink blocking tests

commit.message:
fix: handle multiple Content-Length values in SSRF size check (#2621)

* fix: handle multiple Content-Length values in SSRF size check

If a server sends duplicate Content-Length headers, the requests library
combines them as comma-separated values (e.g., "100, 10485761"). The
previous int() parse would raise ValueError, silently bypassing the
size check. Now splits on commas, rejects conflicting values per
RFC 7230 §3.3.2, and normalizes identical duplicates.

Adds 5 edge-case tests for comma-separated, zero, and negative
Content-Length values.

* fix: filter empty parts in Content-Length multi-value parsing

Trailing or doubled commas (e.g. "10485761," or "100,,200") produced
empty strings that caused int("") to raise ValueError, which was
silently caught — bypassing the size check entirely. Filter empty
parts before int() conversion so oversized values are still rejected
and conflicting values are still detected.

Also adds 6 regression tests for empty-part edge cases and hardens
existing tests with status_code=200 and close() assertions.

commit.message:
fix: stop linking FAILED documents to Library collection (#2640)

FAILED Document records are still created for tracking/audit purposes,
but they are no longer linked to the Library collection. This prevents
DB clutter since the Library UI already filters to status=="completed"
making FAILED docs invisible but wasteful.

Also removes the defense-in-depth is_downloadable_url guard that is no
longer needed since no FAILED docs get linked to Library regardless.

commit.message:
fix: filter non-downloadable sources from Library to prevent pollution (#2627)

Move is_downloadable_domain() from library_routes.py to utils/__init__.py
as the single source of truth. Add is_downloadable_url() wrapper.

- Scheduler: filter resources before calling download_as_text()
- download_all_text route: filter to only downloadable resources
- _is_downloadable(): delegate to consolidated is_downloadable_url()
- _record_failed_text_extraction(): skip Library entries for non-downloadable URLs

Previously, every research source was processed by the scheduler,
causing non-downloadable URLs (blogs, news, Wikipedia) to create
FAILED Document records in the Library collection.

commit.message:
improve: route APScheduler logs through loguru (#2624)

* improve: route APScheduler logs through loguru via InterceptHandler

APScheduler's logger hierarchy had no InterceptHandler, so job execution
messages bypassed loguru entirely — INFO-level logs were invisible, and
WARNING/ERROR only reached Python's lastResort handler as unformatted
stderr. Add InterceptHandler to the "apscheduler" parent logger at
WARNING level, matching the existing werkzeug pattern.

Also add handler duplication guards to both werkzeug and apscheduler
logger setup to prevent handler accumulation when create_app() is called
multiple times (e.g. in tests).

* fix: correct inaccurate code comment about INFO-level visibility

fix: followup improvements from PR #2618 review (#2626)

- Replace bare db_manager.get_session() with get_user_db_session() context manager (queue/manager)

The PR changed manager.py to use get_user_db_session instead of
db_manager, but tests still mocked the old db_manager name. Updated
all 12 tests to mock get_user_db_session as a context manager and
changed "no connection" tests to expect DatabaseSessionError.

* fix: minor robustness improvements from PR #2595 review

- Merge double DB session in verify_file() into single context block
- Add try-except around notify_research_queued to prevent masking success
- Add thread-safe double-checked locking for global search cache init
- Delete stale .pyc files from removed research_routes_orm

* fix: return error on reset_to_defaults failure, elevate log level, clear disposed cache

- reset_to_defaults now returns 500 error instead of success when
  load_from_defaults_file() raises an exception
- Elevate notify_research_queued failure log from debug to warning
- Set _global_cache = None after dispose to prevent returning disposed instance

commit.message:
fix: remove dead `allow_redirects` parameter from `validate_url()` (#2620)

The parameter was accepted but never read in the function body. No
callers pass `allow_redirects` to `validate_url()` — redirect
validation is handled at the caller level (safe_get/safe_post manual
loop and SafeSession.send() override). Also removes the vulture
whitelist entry that suppressed the dead-code warning.

commit.message:
feat: move security hardening to security/ module (#2594)

* feat: move security hardening to security/ module

Add account lockout (per-user), password strength validation, and log
sanitization as proper modules under security/. Wire them into auth
routes for login, registration, and password change. Add session fixation
prevention (session.clear() before new session creation).

Supersedes #1955.

* fix: make lockout settings non-editable and hidden from UI

Security-critical settings should only be configurable via environment
variables, not through the settings UI.

* ci: whitelist password_validator.py in filename check

The file is a password strength validator, not a password store.

* fix: log_sanitizer truncation respects max_length, fix missed password

- Truncation now produces exactly max_length chars (not max_length+3)
- Fix misleading comment about unicode handling
- Fix missed weak password in test_research_api_debug.py

* fix: update missed weak password in api_tests_with_login conftest

* feat: add logging, memory protection, and multi-process docs to account lockout

- Add loguru logging for lock/unlock/expiry events (without usernames)
- Add _MAX_STATE_ENTRIES guard to prevent unbounded memory growth
- Document per-process limitation for multi-worker deployments
- Add test for state clearing when max entries exceeded

- Extract _DEFAULTS dict to avoid duplicating default values

* revert: keep dismiss_* settings visible in Settings UI

The visible: false change would prevent users from re-enabling
dismissed warnings. Reverted to keep the PR focused.

- Remove redundant nested context managers (settings_routes reset_to_defaults)
- Add atexit shutdown hooks for thread engine and search cache cleanup (app_factory, search_cache)
- Delete dead code research_routes_orm.py and its tests (never imported, blueprint never registered)

* fix: update queue manager tests to mock get_user_db_session context manager

The PR changed manager.py to use get_user_db_session instead of
db_manager, but tests still mocked the old db_manager name. Updated
all 12 tests to mock get_user_db_session as a context manager and
changed "no connection" tests to expect DatabaseSessionError.

* test: add 323 high-value tests across 10 modules (round 9)

- strategies/constrained_search: restrictiveness ranking, query generation
- metrics/token_counter: TokenCountingCallback init, start, end, error
- rate_limiting/tracker: AdaptiveRateLimitTracker profiles, estimates

* fix: correct 3 test issues found during review

- small_utilities: remove out-of-contract None test for str->str function,
  consolidate with existing empty string test
- thread_settings: use exact assertion (== "42") instead of ambiguous
  in (42, "42") since ui_element="text" always converts to str
- topic: fix misleading comment about merge behavior — only supporting
  sources are merged, not lead source

* fix: address code review findings for high-value tests round 9

Production bug fixes:
- topic.py: merge_topics now preserves topic2.lead_source
- topic.py: update_lead_source uses add_supporting_source to prevent duplicates
- json_utils.py: ellipsis regex preserves comma separator between items

- Fix 5 false-positive tests that passed regardless of code correctness
- Strengthen weak assertions (json.loads checks, exact equality, type checks)
- Remove 30+ duplicated tests from test_search_utilities_high_value.py
- Add 5 context overflow tests to match docstring claims
- Add tests for production bugs (merge_topics, update_lead_source duplicates)
- Fix misleading test names and comments
- Add random seed for statistical test determinism
- Verify mock interactions in Jinja render-error test

key instead of the child key to get_typed_setting_value. This was harmless

only, not default settings definitions.

existing domain-agnostic approach that extracts terms from constraint.value.

both _validate_search_results and _quick_evidence_check to prevent
crashes on non-string values.

- Wrap constraint.value with str() in _format_search_summary to prevent
  slice crash on non-string values
- Add return value assertion to test_statistic_numeric_value_no_crash

  and should not be changed to "is not None"

commit.message:
security: add path traversal protection for file reads (#1890)

* security: add path traversal protection for file reads

Add PathValidator.validate_safe_path() checks when resolving relative
paths to absolute paths for file reads. This prevents path traversal
attacks where malicious file_path values could read files outside the
library directory.

Fixed locations:
- PDFStorageManager: has_pdf, _load_from_filesystem, delete_pdf
- DownloadService: _try_extract_from_existing_pdf
- utils: get_absolute_library_path, get_absolute_path_from_settings

* fix: apply ruff formatting

* fix: standardize error handling for path traversal protection

Address AI code review feedback by making error handling consistent:

- Change utility functions to return Optional[Path] instead of raising
  ValueError, matching PDFStorageManager._get_safe_file_path() pattern
- Update all callers in library_service.py and document_deletion.py to
  handle None return values safely
- Add _get_safe_absolute_path helper to LibraryService for cleaner code
- Prevents crashes when path traversal is blocked, instead gracefully
  handling invalid paths

This makes the security protection non-breaking for existing code while
still logging and blocking path traversal attempts.

* test: add comprehensive tests for path traversal protection

Add 24 tests covering:
- get_absolute_library_path: valid paths, traversal attempts, edge cases
- get_absolute_path_from_settings: valid paths, traversal attempts
- LibraryService._get_safe_absolute_path: markers, edge cases, traversal
- PDFStorageManager._get_safe_file_path: all path scenarios
- Integration tests: has_pdf, _load_from_filesystem, delete_pdf

These tests verify that malicious file_path values in the database cannot
be used to read or delete files outside the library directory.

* fix: handle None in is_already_downloaded path check

Found by code review: is_already_downloaded() could crash with
AttributeError when absolute_path was None (path traversal blocked).

Changes:
- Add None check before calling .exists() on absolute_path
- Only mark tracker as not downloaded if path exists but file doesn't
- Add test case for malicious path in download tracker

* test: add comprehensive path traversal attack vector tests

Add 16 more tests covering:
- Various path traversal attack patterns (simple .., nested, absolute)
- Edge cases (whitespace, special chars, unicode, long paths)
- CascadeHelper path safety (special markers, None, empty, file ops)
- Verification that traversal staying within root is allowed

Total: 41 tests for path traversal protection

* test: add OWASP-recommended path traversal test vectors

Based on OWASP and PayloadsAllTheThings research, add tests for:
- Windows-style backslash traversal (platform-aware)
- Mixed slash patterns with real traversal
- Mangled patterns (..../) - verified they stay in library
- Null byte injection (%00)
- UNC path injection (Windows network shares)
- Dot segment variations (..;/ Tomcat bypass)

Total: 47 tests for path traversal protection

Sources:
- OWASP Path Traversal: https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Path_Traversal
- OWASP Testing Guide: https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide
- PayloadsAllTheThings: https://github.com/swisskyrepo/PayloadsAllTheThings

* test: add tests for PDF storage path traversal protection

Tests _validate_filesystem_path blocks traversal, symlinks, and sentinel
values. Verifies library_root is resolved and get_absolute_path_from_settings
rejects paths escaping library root.

* fix: resolve 5 test bugs in path traversal protection

- Rename _validate_filesystem_path → _get_safe_file_path (method name mismatch)
- Change test_traversal_raises_value_error to test_traversal_returns_none (function returns None, doesn't raise)
- Resolve library_root on init for symlink defense-in-depth
- Add symlink detection in _get_safe_file_path (werkzeug safe_join only checks string traversal)
- Fix PR reference in docstring (#1943 → #1890)

* fix: correct 2 test bugs found in post-fix review of path traversal protection

Bug 6: inspect.getsource target updated from has_pdf to _get_safe_file_path
since sentinel strings were moved during refactor.

Bug 7: Added file_type="pdf" and DocumentBlob mock to has_pdf path traversal
test so it exercises the actual path validation code instead of short-circuiting
at the file_type check.

* fix: resolve 3 low-severity bugs found in review round 2

Bug 8: _infer_storage_mode now excludes 'text_only_not_stored' sentinel
Bug 9: test_null_byte_injection asserts None instead of permissive check
Bug 10: Remove dead code after validate_safe_path (always raises on failure)

commit.message:
feat: auto-close idle database connections on session expiry (#2592)

* fix: properly dispose db engines in rate limiting test teardown

The temp_data_dir cleanup (shutil.rmtree) was failing with
OSError: Directory not empty because db_manager.connections.clear()
only removed dict entries without disposing SQLAlchemy engines,
leaving database files locked. Now explicitly dispose each engine
and clean up thread engines before clearing connections.

Fixes CI error in test_successful_login_still_counts_toward_limit.

* refactor: extract _dispose_db_manager_connections helper

DRY up the duplicated engine disposal logic into a shared function
used by both test class fixtures.

* fix: add close_all_databases() to db_manager, replace bare connections.clear()

connections.clear() only removed dict entries without disposing
SQLAlchemy engines, leaking file locks and causing OSError on
temp directory cleanup. Added close_all_databases() method to
EncryptedDatabaseManager that properly disposes all engines and
cleans up thread engines. Replaced all 11 bare connections.clear()
calls across test fixtures with the new method.

* fix: register atexit handler to close all db connections on shutdown

db_manager had no shutdown hook — database engines were never
disposed on server exit, leaking file locks. Added close_all_databases()
to EncryptedDatabaseManager and registered it as an atexit handler
alongside the existing scheduler and log-flush handlers.

* feat: auto-close idle database connections on session expiry

Database connections opened on login/registration were only closed on
explicit logout. When users close their browser without logging out,
SQLAlchemy engines (with QueuePool of up to 40 connections) stayed open
indefinitely — leaking memory, holding SQLCipher file locks, and
accumulating until process restart.

Changes:
- Add threading.Lock to SessionManager for thread-safe session access
- Add get_active_usernames() and has_active_sessions_for() to SessionManager
- Move session_manager singleton to session_manager.py (re-exported from routes)
- Remove gc.collect() from destroy_session() (no security benefit)
- Add get_usernames_with_active_research() to globals.py
- Add get_connected_usernames() to DatabaseManager
- Create connection_cleanup.py with APScheduler-based periodic cleanup
- Wire up cleanup scheduler in app.py with atexit shutdown
- Add 11 tests covering cleanup logic, race conditions, and thread safety

* fix: harden connection cleanup scheduler against edge cases

- Guard against duplicate schedulers in Flask debug reloader mode
- Wrap scheduler startup in try/except so app doesn't crash if
  APScheduler fails (matches news_scheduler pattern)
- Re-check active research in double-check loop to close TOCTOU gap
- Add test for research race condition in double-check phase
- Add WERKZEUG_RUN_MAIN to allowed system env vars in pre-commit hook

* fix: harden idle connection cleanup resilience and credential hygiene

- Make close_user_database() always delete engine from dict even if
  dispose() raises, preventing zombie engines that retry every 5 min
- Wrap per-user close in try/except so one failure doesn't abort the
  loop for remaining users
- Clear session passwords when idle connections are cleaned up,
  matching the logout path behavior
- Add resilience test verifying loop continues after close failure
- Remove duplicate test_destroy_valid_session_removes_it

* fix: unregister news scheduler jobs during idle connection cleanup

The idle cleanup path was closing database connections without
unregistering the user from the news scheduler, leaving orphaned
APScheduler jobs that would re-open the database on next trigger.
Now mirrors the logout pattern in routes.py by calling
scheduler.unregister_user() before close_user_database().

Also adds exc_info=True to the cleanup scheduler startup warning
in app.py so exception tracebacks are preserved for diagnostics.

* fix: add WERKZEUG_RUN_MAIN to CI test SYSTEM_VARS

The pre-commit hook already had this var but the CI test's
SYSTEM_VARS set was missing it, causing check-env-vars to fail.

commit.message:
test: add 53 high-value tests across 3 modules (round 12) (#2600)

* test: add 53 high-value tests across 3 modules (round 12)

New test modules:
- tests/web/routes/test_metrics_analytics_logic.py (27 tests)
  _extract_domain() URL normalization, get_rating_analytics() and
  get_link_analytics() pure aggregation logic with mocked DB
- tests/database/test_credential_store_ttl.py (10 tests)
  TTL boundary conditions (exact expiry), remove parameter,
  _cleanup_expired with mocked time.time()
- tests/research_library/services/test_pdf_filename_generation.py (16 tests)
  _generate_filename() edge cases (arXiv/PubMed without valid IDs,
  fallback paths), _infer_storage_mode() attribute/value edge cases

Modules 2 (settings parsing) and 3 (encrypted DB cleanup) from the
original plan were skipped — cross-verification found they already have
comprehensive coverage in test_manager_behavior.py,
test_settings_manager.py, and test_thread_engine_management.py.

* refactor: move imports to module level, use tmp_path fixture

Address review feedback:
- Move imports to module level in test_metrics_analytics_logic.py
- Replace hardcoded /tmp/test_lib with pytest tmp_path fixture in
  test_pdf_filename_generation.py
- Extract helper functions to module level for cleaner test classes

* fix: remove 8 duplicate tests, fix vacuous test, strengthen assertions

- Remove TestRetrieveRemoveParameter class (3 tests) from credential_store_ttl
  duplicated in test_credential_store_extended.py
- Remove test_no_expired_entries duplicated in test_credential_store_extended.py
- Remove test_non_arxiv_non_pmc_returns_fallback from pdf_filename_generation
  duplicated in test_pdf_storage_manager.py
- Remove test_document_with_blob_returns_database, _with_file_path_returns_filesystem,
  _metadata_only_returns_none duplicated in test_pdf_storage_manager.py
- Fix test_some_expired: bypass _store_credentials to avoid implicit cleanup
  making the explicit _cleanup_expired() call vacuously satisfied
- Strengthen weak assertions: len(top) >= 1 → == 2, <= 10 → == 10

commit.message:
docs: thread lifecycle, FD budget, and resource exhaustion (#2605)

* fix: prevent file descriptor exhaustion from dead thread engine accumulation

Three root causes addressed:

1. Dead thread engine accumulation (primary): _thread_engines grows
   unboundedly as crashed/terminated threads leave orphaned NullPool
   engines. Add cleanup_dead_thread_engines() that sweeps entries for
   threads no longer in threading.enumerate(). Integrate via throttled
   sweep in teardown_appcontext (every 60s) and periodic sweep in the
   queue processor loop (every 6 iterations).

2. Generic downloader stream=True leak (secondary): generic.py used
   stream=True but never read or closed the response body, holding
   connections open. Removed stream=True since only status_code and
   headers are inspected.

3. Docker default 1024 FD limit (contributing): Add nofile ulimit
   (65536) to docker-compose.yml so the container has headroom for
   WAL mode databases, thread pools, and connection pools.

* fix: address review findings — sweep lock, credential cleanup, flaky test

- Add _sweep_lock to prevent TOCTOU race on _last_sweep_time in
  maybe_sweep_dead_engines() (concurrent teardowns could all pass the
  interval check)
- Move alive_ids computation inside _thread_engine_lock to prevent
  race between snapshot and engine dict mutation
- Sweep dead _thread_credentials (plaintext passwords) alongside engines
  in processor_v2.py and app_factory.py teardown
- Fix flaky test_sweeps_after_interval: replace time.sleep(0.15) with
  _last_sweep_time backdating
- Add tests for credential sweep and module-level cleanup_dead_threads()

* fix: close search engine sessions after research, fix stream=True leak properly

Three improvements to the FD exhaustion fix:

1. generic.py: Restore stream=True (removing it is unsafe — GenericDownloader
   handles ALL URLs and would download multi-GB files into memory). Use context
   manager instead to ensure the streamed connection is properly closed on all
   return paths, preventing socket FD leaks.

2. research_service.py: Add use_search.close() and system.close() in finally
   block of run_research_process(). Search engine HTTP sessions (e.g.
   SemanticScholar's SafeSession) were never explicitly closed after research,
   relying on non-deterministic GC for cleanup.

3. search_system.py + strategies: Add close() method to AdvancedSearchSystem
   and BaseSearchStrategy, with overrides in ConstraintParallelStrategy and
   ConcurrentDualConfidenceStrategy to shut down persistent ThreadPoolExecutors.

Also adds detailed design comments throughout the codebase documenting:
- Why NullPool engines don't leak FDs (memory leak only)
- Why stream=True must NOT be removed from the diagnostic block
- The dual sweep trigger architecture (request-driven + queue-driven)
- Thread ID recycling limitations
- Search engine lifecycle and cleanup responsibilities

Fixes flaky test_removes_dead_thread_entries by using threading.Barrier to
prevent thread ID recycling during test.

* fix: unregister user from news scheduler on logout

The logout handler never called scheduler.unregister_user(), causing:
- Passwords to persist in scheduler memory for up to 48 hours
- Orphaned APScheduler jobs to keep running after logout
- Orphaned jobs to re-create QueuePool engines (~10 FDs each) after
  close_user_database() disposed the original, contributing to FD leaks

Add scheduler unregistration before close_user_database() so running
jobs can finish gracefully while the DB engine is still available.
Add design comment documenting the logout cleanup order.

* test: remove ineffective patch in logout scheduler test

The `routes.get_news_scheduler` patch was ineffective because the logout
handler imports `get_news_scheduler` dynamically inside the function body,
so the name never enters the routes module namespace. The `create=True`
flag masked this by silently creating a new attribute. The real patch on
`subscription_manager.scheduler.get_news_scheduler` is sufficient.

* fix: remove nofile ulimit override from docker-compose.yml

Docker containers inherit ulimits from the Docker daemon, which typically
runs with LimitNOFILE=infinity (1073741816+). Setting nofile to 65536
could actually *lower* the limit for most users, hurting large
installations. The FD leak root causes are already fixed in this PR
(dead-thread engine sweep, session close, scheduler unregister), so the
safety net is unnecessary. Let users and their Docker daemon config
control this.

* fix: add try-except to strategy executor shutdown, elevate scheduler unregister log level

- Wrap executor.shutdown(wait=False) in try-except in strategy close()
  methods for consistency with parallel_search_engine.py pattern
- Change logger.debug → logger.warning for scheduler unregister failure
  on logout, since failure means password stays in scheduler memory

* docs: add comments explaining non-obvious design decisions from deep review

- SQLCipher WAL FD cost (1-3 FDs per connection, multiplied by users)
- Logout cleanup ordering: why unregister before close, known race window
- shutdown(wait=False): why non-blocking, safety via double-cleanup pattern

* docs: add thread lifecycle, FD budget, and resource exhaustion documentation

Knowledge captured from PR #2591 deep review (5 rounds of verification):
- architecture.md: Thread & Resource Lifecycle section with cleanup layers,
  mermaid diagram, FD budget table, and key files reference
- troubleshooting.md: Resource Exhaustion section with diagnosis commands
  and solutions for FD exhaustion
- docker-compose-guide.md: Resource Limits note explaining nofile/memlock
- web/database/README.md: Thread Safety & Connection Model section
- Cross-references added between all 4 docs
- Updated Areas for Improvement (container optimization → resource observability)
- Added encrypted_db.py and thread_local_session.py to Key Source Files

commit.message:
refactor: eliminate server_config.json — env-var-only server settings (#2505)

* refactor: eliminate server_config.json, make server settings env-var-only

Remove the JSON file-based server configuration and sync mechanism.
All 8 server settings (host, port, debug, HTTPS, allow_registrations,
and 3 rate limits) are now read exclusively from environment variables
via get_typed_setting_value() with the existing LDR_* naming convention.

- Rewrite server_config.py: remove get_server_config_path(),
  save_server_config(), sync_from_settings(); simplify load_server_config()
  to use get_typed_setting_value(key, None, ...) for all settings
- Add rate_limit_settings to the config dict (was only via .get() fallback)
- Remove sync_from_settings calls from 3 sites in settings_routes.py
- Hide server settings from UI (visible: false, editable: false) in
  default_settings.json and settings_security.json
- Add security.rate_limit_settings entry to settings_security.json
- Fix swapped min/max on web.port (was min:65535, max:0)
- Update descriptions to reference env var names
- Rewrite test_server_config.py: remove 21 JSON-file tests, keep 13
  defaults/fail-closed tests, add 8 env var override tests (35 total)
- Regenerate golden master settings
- Remove server_config.py from check-file-writes.sh exemption list
- Update docstrings in rate_limiter.py and app.py

* fix: address review findings for server_config.json elimination

- Fix save_all_settings response: return dict (keyed by setting key)
  instead of list, matching GET /settings/api shape; include missing
  visible, min_value, max_value, step fields so visibility filter works
- Fix JS consumer: use dict key access instead of .find() on response
- Fix docs: LDR_WEB_PORT is the correct env var for server bind port,
  not LDR_APP_PORT; add clarifying note
- Remove stale KNOWN_NUMERIC_ISSUES entry for web.port (now fixed)
- Add tests: empty-string and whitespace env var edge cases for
  allow_registrations fail-closed, and env-var override coverage

* feat: add deprecation migration path for server_config.json (#2549)

Users who set `allow_registrations: false` via the UI (persisted in
server_config.json) would silently lose that setting on upgrade,
re-enabling open registration. Docker users are especially at risk
since named volumes persist the file across container upgrades.

Add read-only migration: if server_config.json exists, honor its
values as fallbacks (env var > legacy file > default) and log
deprecation warnings guiding users to migrate to env vars.

No write-back logic is re-added — save_server_config() and
sync_from_settings() remain removed per the PR's intent.

* feat: show web UI warning when legacy server_config.json is detected

Adds a dismissible warning banner in the web interface when the
deprecated server_config.json file exists, using the existing
warning_checks system. Addresses reviewer feedback from PR #2505.

* fix: address review findings for server_config.json elimination

- Change web.host, web.port, web.use_https type from SEARCH to APP
  in both default_settings.json and golden_master_settings.json
- Add 3 tests for check_legacy_server_config() covering dismissed,
  missing file, and file-exists branches
- Add autouse fixture to clear LDR_APP_ALLOW_REGISTRATIONS env var
  in test_server_config.py to prevent test pollution from dev shell

* fix: round 2 review findings for server_config.json elimination

- Fix flaky test_all_four_warnings_simultaneously by mocking
  get_server_config_path to prevent real server_config.json on disk
  from breaking exact set equality assertion
- Add dismiss_legacy_config to _make_settings_manager defaults and
  rename test_all_six_settings_read → test_all_seven_settings_read
- Add orchestrator-level tests for legacy_server_config warning
  (exists/absent/dismissed scenarios)
- Add fail-closed guard for legacy JSON allow_registrations string
  values (e.g. "disabled" → False) to match env var guard
- Log warning for unrecognized keys in legacy server_config.json
  to surface typos like "Port" instead of "port"
- Regenerate CONFIGURATION.md to remove stale server_config.json
  reference in app.debug description

* fix: round 3 review findings — test quality and migration docs

- Replace vacuous `is not None` assertion with meaningful env-var-vs-legacy
  guard priority test using unrecognized values on both paths
- Add positive test for DEPRECATED banner when recognized keys present
- Rename misleading test name to reflect actual scope (hardware + context)
- Add migration section to env_configuration.md for server_config.json users

commit.message:
security: validate redirect targets in SSRF-protected HTTP requests (#1949)

* security: validate redirect targets in safe_get() and SafeSession

When allow_redirects=True, manually follow redirects and validate
each redirect target against SSRF rules. Previously, the initial URL
was validated but redirect targets were not — an attacker's server
could redirect to internal services (localhost, cloud metadata, etc).

This protects all callers including download_service.py and base.py
which pass allow_redirects=True for PDF downloads.

* fix: add redirect interception to safe_post() and fix size-check bug

Three changes:
1. Move urljoin import to module level instead of inline imports
2. Apply the same SSRF-validated redirect interception to safe_post()
   that safe_get() already has — previously a caller could override
   allow_redirects=True and bypass SSRF checks on redirect targets
3. Fix size-check bug where except(ValueError) swallowed the
   intentional ValueError raise for oversized responses — use
   try/except/else pattern matching safe_get()

* test: add tests for SSRF-validated redirect following

Verifies manual redirect following validates each hop against SSRF
rules, blocks redirects to internal IPs, and enforces max redirect limit.

* security: fix SSRF response size check being silently bypassed (with tests) (#2100)

* security: fix SSRF response size check being silently bypassed

The ValueError raised when Content-Length exceeds MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE
was caught by the same except (ValueError, TypeError) block meant
for int() parsing failures. Use try/except/else to separate the
int conversion failure from the size validation.

* test: add tests for SSRF response size check try/except/else pattern

Verifies Content-Length validation correctly raises ValueError for
oversized responses while silently ignoring non-numeric values.

* fix: implement RFC 7231 redirect method conversion in SSRF-validated redirect following

The manual redirect loop in safe_post() and SafeSession.request() always
re-used the original HTTP method, breaking POST→302→GET flows (e.g. login)
and dropping request bodies on 307/308 redirects.

- Add _resolve_redirect_method() helper matching requests library behavior
- safe_post(): convert POST to GET on 301/302/303 (drop body), preserve
  POST with body on 307/308
- SafeSession.request(): same method conversion + kwargs.pop for body cleanup
- safe_get(): add current_url tracking for better error logging
- Update 2 existing tests for the size-check bug fix (try/except/else)
- Update test_can_enable_redirects for new redirect interception behavior
- Add 20 new tests: helper unit tests, method conversion, SafeSession redirects

* fix: correct SSRF redirect validation defaults, RFC compliance, and resource leaks

- Change allow_redirects default from False to True to match requests library behavior
- Fix 302 method conversion to only apply POST→GET per RFC 7231 §6.4.3
- Guard urljoin against response.url being None
- Close intermediate redirect responses to prevent connection leaks
- Add missing safe_post redirect tests (too many redirects, missing Location)

* fix: close leaked responses on SSRF/redirect/size errors, add SafeSession size check

- Close response before raising on SSRF validation failure, too-many-redirects,
  and oversized Content-Length (C1/C2/C3 resource leaks)
- Extract _check_response_size() helper to deduplicate size checks (M3)
- Add Content-Length check to SafeSession.send() (H1 — parity with safe_get/safe_post)
- Strip whitespace from Location header to avoid opaque urljoin errors (H2)
- Rename misleading test_disables_redirects_by_default to clarify that
  requests-level disable is intentional for manual SSRF-validated following (C4)
- Add test_can_enable_redirects for safe_post (H3)
- Add tests for: response.close() on intermediate/error paths, POST body
  not forwarded on 302/303 conversion, per-hop SSRF validation in safe_post,
  relative redirect URL resolution, response.url=None fallback,
  SafeSession Content-Length rejection (H4)
- Fix M2 comment: "preserve method" → "preserve current method"
- Add M1 design comment to SafeSession documenting resolve_redirects reliance

* fix: harden SSRF redirect tests and minor code clarity improvements

- Use elif chain in _resolve_redirect_method to prevent fragile fall-through
- Clarify _check_response_size docstring re: chunked encoding limitation
- Add inline comment in SafeSession.send() noting intentional size-check difference
- Add test for whitespace stripping on Location header
- Replace vacuously true body-drop tests with multi-hop POST→302→GET→307 scenario
- Strengthen hop-validation tests to assert exact per-hop URLs passed to validate_url

* fix: align SafeSession redirect limits, skip 3xx size checks, add SSRF logging

- Set SafeSession.max_redirects = _MAX_REDIRECTS (10) to match safe_get/safe_post
- Skip _check_response_size for 3xx redirect hops (bodies are discarded anyway)
- Add logger.warning before raising ValueError on SSRF redirect blocks
- Update SafeSession docstrings: Raises section, send() defense-in-depth note
- Add test for default allow_redirects behavior (no explicit kwarg)
- Improve test_send_handles_none_url docstring explaining defensive guard

* fix: harden SafeSession size check, add SSRF logging, and expand test coverage

- Only skip size check for 3xx responses that will actually be followed
  (have Location header and allow_redirects=True); a 3xx with no Location
  or allow_redirects=False is the final response and must be size-checked
- Add logger.warning() to SafeSession.send() SSRF block for audit parity
  with safe_get/safe_post
- Reject negative Content-Length values as malformed in _check_response_size
- Strengthen test_default_allow_redirects_follows with call_count assertion
- Add safe_post response-close tests (SSRF, too-many-redirects, oversized)
- Add tests verifying 300/304 are NOT followed as redirects

1. Move urljoin import to module level instead of inline imports
2. Apply the same SSRF-validated redirect interception to safe_post()
   that safe_get() already has — previously a caller could override
   allow_redirects=True and bypass SSRF checks on redirect targets
3. Fix size-check bug where except(ValueError) swallowed the
   intentional ValueError raise for oversized responses — use
   try/except/else pattern matching safe_get()

* test: add tests for SSRF-validated redirect following

Verifies manual redirect following validates each hop against SSRF
rules, blocks redirects to internal IPs, and enforces max redirect limit.

* security: fix SSRF response size check being silently bypassed (with tests) (#2100)

* security: fix SSRF response size check being silently bypassed

The ValueError raised when Content-Length exceeds MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE
was caught by the same except (ValueError, TypeError) block meant
for int() parsing failures. Use try/except/else to separate the
int conversion failure from the size validation.

* test: add tests for SSRF response size check try/except/else pattern

Verifies Content-Length validation correctly raises ValueError for
oversized responses while silently ignoring non-numeric values.

* fix: implement RFC 7231 redirect method conversion in SSRF-validated redirect following

The manual redirect loop in safe_post() and SafeSession.request() always
re-used the original HTTP method, breaking POST→302→GET flows (e.g. login)
and dropping request bodies on 307/308 redirects.

- safe_post(): convert POST to GET on 301/302/303 (drop body), preserve
  POST with body on 307/308
- SafeSession.request(): same method conversion + kwargs.pop for body cleanup
- safe_get(): add current_url tracking for better error logging
- Update 2 existing tests for the size-check bug fix (try/except/else)
- Update test_can_enable_redirects for new redirect interception behavior
- Add 20 new tests: helper unit tests, method conversion, SafeSession redirects

* fix: correct SSRF redirect validation defaults, RFC compliance, and resource leaks

- Change allow_redirects default from False to True to match requests library behavior
- Fix 302 method conversion to only apply POST→GET per RFC 7231 §6.4.3
- Guard urljoin against response.url being None
- Close intermediate redirect responses to prevent connection leaks
- Add missing safe_post redirect tests (too many redirects, missing Location)

* fix: close leaked responses on SSRF/redirect/size errors, add SafeSession size check

- Close response before raising on SSRF validation failure, too-many-redirects,
  and oversized Content-Length (C1/C2/C3 resource leaks)
- Extract _check_response_size() helper to deduplicate size checks (M3)
- Add Content-Length check to SafeSession.send() (H1 — parity with safe_get/safe_post)
- Strip whitespace from Location header to avoid opaque urljoin errors (H2)
- Rename misleading test_disables_redirects_by_default to clarify that
  requests-level disable is intentional for manual SSRF-validated following (C4)
- Add test_can_enable_redirects for safe_post (H3)
- Add tests for: response.close() on intermediate/error paths, POST body
  not forwarded on 302/303 conversion, per-hop SSRF validation in safe_post,
  relative redirect URL resolution, response.url=None fallback,
  SafeSession Content-Length rejection (H4)
- Fix M2 comment: "preserve method" → "preserve current method"
- Add M1 design comment to SafeSession documenting resolve_redirects reliance

* fix: harden SSRF redirect tests and minor code clarity improvements

- Use elif chain in _resolve_redirect_method to prevent fragile fall-through
- Clarify _check_response_size docstring re: chunked encoding limitation
- Add inline comment in SafeSession.send() noting intentional size-check difference
- Add test for whitespace stripping on Location header
- Replace vacuously true body-drop tests with multi-hop POST→302→GET→307 scenario
- Strengthen hop-validation tests to assert exact per-hop URLs passed to validate_url

* fix: align SafeSession redirect limits, skip 3xx size checks, add SSRF logging

- Set SafeSession.max_redirects = _MAX_REDIRECTS (10) to match safe_get/safe_post
- Skip _check_response_size for 3xx redirect hops (bodies are discarded anyway)
- Add logger.warning before raising ValueError on SSRF redirect blocks
- Update SafeSession docstrings: Raises section, send() defense-in-depth note
- Add test for default allow_redirects behavior (no explicit kwarg)
- Improve test_send_handles_none_url docstring explaining defensive guard

* fix: harden SafeSession size check, add SSRF logging, and expand test coverage

- Only skip size check for 3xx responses that will actually be followed
  (have Location header and allow_redirects=True); a 3xx with no Location
  or allow_redirects=False is the final response and must be size-checked
- Add logger.warning() to SafeSession.send() SSRF block for audit parity
  with safe_get/safe_post
- Reject negative Content-Length values as malformed in _check_response_size
- Strengthen test_default_allow_redirects_follows with call_count assertion
- Add safe_post response-close tests (SSRF, too-many-redirects, oversized)
- Add tests verifying 300/304 are NOT followed as redirects

commit.message:
fix: prevent file descriptor exhaustion (too many open files) (#2591)

* fix: prevent file descriptor exhaustion from dead thread engine accumulation

Three root causes addressed:

1. Dead thread engine accumulation (primary): _thread_engines grows
   unboundedly as crashed/terminated threads leave orphaned NullPool
   engines. Add cleanup_dead_thread_engines() that sweeps entries for
   threads no longer in threading.enumerate(). Integrate via throttled
   sweep in teardown_appcontext (every 60s) and periodic sweep in the
   queue processor loop (every 6 iterations).

2. Generic downloader stream=True leak (secondary): generic.py used
   stream=True but never read or closed the response body, holding
   connections open. Removed stream=True since only status_code and
   headers are inspected.

3. Docker default 1024 FD limit (contributing): Add nofile ulimit
   (65536) to docker-compose.yml so the container has headroom for
   WAL mode databases, thread pools, and connection pools.

* fix: address review findings — sweep lock, credential cleanup, flaky test

- Add _sweep_lock to prevent TOCTOU race on _last_sweep_time in
  maybe_sweep_dead_engines() (concurrent teardowns could all pass the
  interval check)
- Move alive_ids computation inside _thread_engine_lock to prevent
  race between snapshot and engine dict mutation
- Sweep dead _thread_credentials (plaintext passwords) alongside engines
  in processor_v2.py and app_factory.py teardown
- Fix flaky test_sweeps_after_interval: replace time.sleep(0.15) with
  _last_sweep_time backdating
- Add tests for credential sweep and module-level cleanup_dead_threads()

* fix: close search engine sessions after research, fix stream=True leak properly

Three improvements to the FD exhaustion fix:

1. generic.py: Restore stream=True (removing it is unsafe — GenericDownloader
   handles ALL URLs and would download multi-GB files into memory). Use context
   manager instead to ensure the streamed connection is properly closed on all
   return paths, preventing socket FD leaks.

2. research_service.py: Add use_search.close() and system.close() in finally
   block of run_research_process(). Search engine HTTP sessions (e.g.
   SemanticScholar's SafeSession) were never explicitly closed after research,
   relying on non-deterministic GC for cleanup.

3. search_system.py + strategies: Add close() method to AdvancedSearchSystem
   and BaseSearchStrategy, with overrides in ConstraintParallelStrategy and
   ConcurrentDualConfidenceStrategy to shut down persistent ThreadPoolExecutors.

Also adds detailed design comments throughout the codebase documenting:
- Why NullPool engines don't leak FDs (memory leak only)
- Why stream=True must NOT be removed from the diagnostic block
- The dual sweep trigger architecture (request-driven + queue-driven)
- Thread ID recycling limitations
- Search engine lifecycle and cleanup responsibilities

Fixes flaky test_removes_dead_thread_entries by using threading.Barrier to
prevent thread ID recycling during test.

* fix: unregister user from news scheduler on logout

The logout handler never called scheduler.unregister_user(), causing:
- Passwords to persist in scheduler memory for up to 48 hours
- Orphaned APScheduler jobs to keep running after logout
- Orphaned jobs to re-create QueuePool engines (~10 FDs each) after
  close_user_database() disposed the original, contributing to FD leaks

Add scheduler unregistration before close_user_database() so running
jobs can finish gracefully while the DB engine is still available.
Add design comment documenting the logout cleanup order.

* test: remove ineffective patch in logout scheduler test

The `routes.get_news_scheduler` patch was ineffective because the logout
handler imports `get_news_scheduler` dynamically inside the function body,
so the name never enters the routes module namespace. The `create=True`
flag masked this by silently creating a new attribute. The real patch on
`subscription_manager.scheduler.get_news_scheduler` is sufficient.

* fix: remove nofile ulimit override from docker-compose.yml

Docker containers inherit ulimits from the Docker daemon, which typically
runs with LimitNOFILE=infinity (1073741816+). Setting nofile to 65536
could actually *lower* the limit for most users, hurting large
installations. The FD leak root causes are already fixed in this PR
(dead-thread engine sweep, session close, scheduler unregister), so the
safety net is unnecessary. Let users and their Docker daemon config
control this.

* fix: add try-except to strategy executor shutdown, elevate scheduler unregister log level

- Wrap executor.shutdown(wait=False) in try-except in strategy close()
  methods for consistency with parallel_search_engine.py pattern
- Change logger.debug → logger.warning for scheduler unregister failure
  on logout, since failure means password stays in scheduler memory

* docs: add comments explaining non-obvious design decisions from deep review

- SQLCipher WAL FD cost (1-3 FDs per connection, multiplied by users)
- Logout cleanup ordering: why unregister before close, known race window
- shutdown(wait=False): why non-blocking, safety via double-cleanup pattern

commit.message:
feat: add LDR_STATIC_MODEL variable for static label research (#2596)

* feat: switch LDR research default model from Gemini Flash to MiniMax M2.5

Update the default OpenRouter model used by the E2E research workflow
from google/gemini-2.0-flash-001 to minimax/minimax-m2.5.

* feat: use LDR_STATIC_MODEL variable for static label research

Use a separate repo variable LDR_STATIC_MODEL for the ldr_research_static
label, allowing a different model (e.g. minimax/minimax-m2.5) for static
regression tests while keeping LDR_MODEL for diff-based research.

* fix: use step output for model selection to avoid falsy value bug

Replace inline GitHub Actions ternary expression with a dedicated step
that correctly handles empty/unset LDR_STATIC_MODEL without falling
through to LDR_MODEL.
